message:
feat(fsfs,ops): extend format emission pipeline and alerts SLO screen

- format_emitter: expand output format adapters for multi-format rendering
- output_schema: refine structured result metadata and score breakdowns
- runtime: extend query execution pipeline with additional stage handling
- ops/app: add new screen routing for alerts and SLO dashboard
- ops/alerts_slo: expand SLO monitoring with threshold configuration
- Sync beads issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2w7x.29","title":"cargo install frankensearch-fsfs works correctly","description":"Ensure 'cargo install frankensearch-fsfs' works from a clean environment. This requires: (1) Cargo.toml metadata: correct name, version, description, license, repository, categories, keywords. (2) All dependencies resolve from crates.io (no path-only deps that would break publish). (3) Build succeeds on stable Rust (or document nightly requirement clearly). (4) The installed binary name should be 'fsfs' (set via [[bin]] name in Cargo.toml). (5) Feature flags should be sensible defaults for cargo install (include download feature for model auto-download). Note: frankensearch uses Rust edition 2024 nightly, so cargo install may require --toolchain nightly. Document this clearly. The path deps to local crates (frankensearch-core, etc.) need to become version deps for crates.io publish — or publish all crates as a workspace.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"QuietGull","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:14:15.768340076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T19:04:22.183369828Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crates-io","feature","packaging","t5-package"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.29","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:14:15.768340076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.29","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:56.939652690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1334,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.29","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design: `cargo install frankensearch-fsfs`

Ensure the crate builds and installs correctly via cargo install. This is the Rust
developer install path (alternative to the curl-bash installer).

## Requirements

1. `Cargo.toml` must have correct `[[bin]]` section:
   ```toml
   [[bin]]
   name = "fsfs"
   path = "src/main.rs"
   ```
2. All dependencies must be published to crates.io OR vendored
3. Build must succeed on stable Rust (if possible) or document nightly requirement
4. The installed binary name is `fsfs` (short, easy to type)

## crates.io Considerations

- Package name: `frankensearch-fsfs` (matches crate name)
- Binary name: `fsfs` (the CLI command name)
- The workspace has internal dependencies (frankensearch-core, etc.) that would
  all need to be published to crates.io for this to work
- Alternative: publish only frankensearch-fsfs with vendored dependencies

## Edition 2024 Blocker

Currently the project uses Rust edition 2024 which requires nightly. This means
`cargo install` requires `cargo +nightly install frankensearch-fsfs`. This is a
friction point. Options:
1. Accept nightly requirement and document it
2. Downgrade to edition 2021 (significant refactoring)
3. Wait for edition 2024 to stabilize (expected late 2026)

Option 1 is the pragmatic choice for now.

## Testing

After implementation, verify:
```bash
cargo install --path crates/frankensearch-fsfs
fsfs --version
fsfs --help
```
","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:25:57Z"},{"id":1391,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.29","author":"QuietGull","text":"Progress update (QuietGull):\n- Updated crate metadata in crates/frankensearch-fsfs/Cargo.toml: stronger description, rust-version inherited from workspace, categories/keywords added for packaging clarity.\n- Added explicit developer install path docs in README.md (cargo +nightly install --path crates/frankensearch-fsfs + basic verification commands).\n- Verified install path works: cargo +nightly install --path crates/frankensearch-fsfs --root /tmp/fsfs-install-quietgull --force (success).\n- Validation: cargo +nightly check -p frankensearch-fsfs passed.\n- Known shared-tree quality-gate blockers still present outside this slice: cargo +nightly clippy -p frankensearch-fsfs --all-targets -- -D warnings fails on existing MSRV/unused_self/too_many_lines/cast lint findings in fsfs runtime/mount_info/agent_ergonomics. cargo +nightly fmt --check also fails on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in other files.","created_at":"2026-02-14T19:04:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2w7x.3","title":"Workspace-wide clippy clean with -D warnings","description":"Ensure cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passes with zero warnings. The codebase uses Rust edition 2024 nightly with forbid(unsafe_code). Clippy is the project's lint standard. Any clippy warnings from new code added by concurrent agents must be fixed. Existing suppression attributes (allow(clippy::too_many_lines) etc.) are acceptable where documented. The fastcma dependency has known warnings — those are external and acceptable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"QuietAnchor","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:10:59.628659672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T17:19:19.558613845Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T17:16:57.981675133Z","close_reason":"Workspace-wide clippy clean: zero warnings across all 12 crates. Only external fastcma dependency has 8 acceptable warnings.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["quality-gate","t0-fix"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:10:59.628659672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:49.373650976Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1307,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Scope

Run `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` and fix every warning.
This is the final T0 hygiene bead before we start building new features.

## Known Warning Categories

From recent sessions, the main clippy warning categories in this workspace are:
- Unused imports (from rapid parallel development by multiple agents)
- Unused variables/fields (features partially implemented)
- Needless borrows (`&String` where `&str` suffices)
- Missing `#[must_use]` on constructors
- `clone()` on Copy types

## Policy

We use `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` project-wide — this must remain. Clippy should run
with `-D warnings` to treat all warnings as errors. This establishes the baseline
quality gate that CI (bd-2w7x.39) will enforce going forward.

## Cross-Crate Considerations

Some crates have `#[allow(dead_code)]` on items that are used by other crates via
public re-exports. Don't remove these items just because they appear unused within
their defining crate — check the facade crate (frankensearch/src/lib.rs) and
frankensearch-fsfs re-exports first.
","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:19:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2w7x.30","title":"Cross-platform release builds (Linux x86/arm, macOS x86/arm)","description":"Set up cross-compilation for the 4 primary targets. This involves: (1) GitHub Actions runners: ubuntu-latest for Linux, macos-latest for macOS. (2) Cross-compilation: use cross or cargo-zigbuild for Linux musl targets, native compilation for macOS. (3) Static linking on Linux (musl) so the binary has zero runtime dependencies. (4) macOS universal binary (optional: lipo to combine x86_64 + aarch64 into one fat binary). (5) ONNX Runtime: this is the biggest cross-compilation challenge — ort (ONNX Runtime for Rust) needs to bundle or link the ONNX Runtime C library. Evaluate: ort with 'download' feature (auto-downloads ONNX Runtime during build) vs bundling a pre-built libonnxruntime. (6) Test the binaries on each platform before release. The ONNX Runtime bundling strategy is the critical design decision here — it determines whether the binary is truly self-contained.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:14:23.120887171Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T17:25:57.301380410Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-compile","feature","release","t5-package"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.30","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:14:23.120887171Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.30","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:57.098883304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1335,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.30","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design: Cross-Platform Release Builds

Set up the build infrastructure to produce binaries for all supported platforms.

## Target Matrix

| Target                        | Build Host    | Toolchain          |
|-------------------------------|---------------|--------------------|
| x86_64-unknown-linux-musl     | Linux x86_64  | Native + musl-tools|
| aarch64-unknown-linux-musl    | Linux x86_64  | cross              |
| x86_64-apple-darwin           | macOS ARM     | Cross-compile      |
| aarch64-apple-darwin          | macOS ARM     | Native             |

## musl for Static Linux Binaries

```bash
# Install musl toolchain
apt-get install musl-tools
rustup target add x86_64-unknown-linux-musl

# Build static binary
RUSTFLAGS="-C target-feature=+crt-static" \
  cargo build --release --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl -p frankensearch-fsfs
```

musl produces fully static binaries — no glibc dependency, no dynamic linking.
This means the binary works on any Linux distribution regardless of glibc version.

## cross for ARM

Use the `cross` tool (Docker-based cross-compilation) for Linux ARM:
```bash
cargo install cross
cross build --release --target aarch64-unknown-linux-musl -p frankensearch-fsfs
```

## ONNX Runtime Challenge

The biggest cross-compilation challenge is ONNX Runtime, which has native C/C++
components. Options:
1. Use `ort` crate with `download` feature (downloads prebuilt ONNX Runtime for target)
2. Vendor ONNX Runtime static libraries per target
3. Build ONNX Runtime from source (slow but most reliable)

The `ort` crate's download feature is the pragmatic choice for CI.

## Binary Size Optimization

- `strip` the binary after compilation
- Use `opt-level = 'z'` for size optimization (or '3' for speed — see bd-3un.1)
- LTO enabled: `lto = true` in profile.release
- `codegen-units = 1` for better optimization

Expected binary size: 20-40MB (ONNX Runtime is the bulk).
","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:25:57Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2w7x.31","title":"Helpful error messages for missing models and dependencies","description":"Every failure mode should produce a clear, actionable error message. Catalog of error scenarios: (1) No index exists: 'No search index found. Run: fsfs index <directory>' (2) Models not downloaded: 'Downloading search models (first time only, ~200MB)...' (3) Model download failed: 'Could not download model X. Check your internet connection or set FRANKENSEARCH_MODEL_DIR to a directory with pre-downloaded models.' (4) ONNX Runtime missing: 'ONNX Runtime not found. Run: fsfs doctor --fix' (5) Corrupt index: 'Search index appears corrupted. Rebuild with: fsfs index --force <directory>' (6) Permission denied: 'Cannot write to X. Try running with --dest or check permissions.' (7) Out of disk space: 'Insufficient disk space. Models require ~200MB, index requires ~X MB.' All errors should use the colored output helpers (err() with red X prefix). Include exit codes: 0=success, 1=general error, 2=usage error, 78=model unavailable, 69=permission denied.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"SunnyCardinal","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:14:30.044078667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T19:06:39.076224960Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["errors","feature","t5-package","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.31","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:14:30.044078667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.31","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:57.256941302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1336,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.31","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design: Helpful Error Messages

Every error the user can encounter should have a clear message explaining what
went wrong and what to do about it.

## Error Categories

### Missing Models
```
error: model 'potion-128m' not found in cache
  expected location: ~/.local/share/frankensearch/models/potion-128m/v1/model.onnx

  To fix: run `fsfs index` with network access to auto-download models
  Or manually: fsfs download-models --model potion-128m
  For offline use: set FRANKENSEARCH_MODEL_DIR to a pre-populated cache
```

### No Index Found
```
error: no search index found
  looked in: /home/user/project/.frankensearch/

  To fix: run `fsfs index /home/user/project` to create an index
```

### Permission Errors
```
error: cannot write to index directory: /home/user/project/.frankensearch/
  reason: Permission denied (os error 13)

  To fix: check directory permissions or use --index-dir to specify an alternative
```

### ONNX Runtime Errors
```
error: failed to load ONNX model: potion-128m
  reason: ONNX Runtime version mismatch (expected >=1.14, found 1.12)

  To fix: update frankensearch to the latest version: fsfs update
```

## Implementation

Use the existing `frankensearch-core/src/error.rs` error types but add user-facing
`Display` implementations that include context and fix suggestions. The `miette`
crate (if added) can provide even nicer error formatting with source spans.

## DCG Pattern

DCG has excellent error messages that always include:
1. What happened (error)
2. Why it matters (context)
3. What to do about it (fix suggestion)

We follow the same three-part pattern for every error.
","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:25:57Z"}]}

Include exit codes: 0=success, 1=general error, 2=usage error, 78=model unavailable, 69=permission denied.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"SunnyCardinal","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:14:30.044078667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T19:13:08.915416820Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T19:13:08.915397715Z","close_reason":"Implemented 3-part error messages (what/context/suggestion) for all SearchError variants. Added suggestion and context fields to OutputError with serde skip_serializing_if. Enhanced emit_table() to render Fix hints. 13 new tests across output_schema and format_emitter. All 678 fsfs tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["errors","feature","t5-package","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.31","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:14:30.044078667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.31","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:57.256941302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1336,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.31","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design: Helpful Error Messages

Every error the user can encounter should have a clear message explaining what
went wrong and what to do about it.

## Error Categories

### Missing Models
```
error: model 'potion-128m' not found in cache
  expected location: ~/.local/share/frankensearch/models/potion-128m/v1/model.onnx

  To fix: run `fsfs index` with network access to auto-download models
  Or manually: fsfs download-models --model potion-128m
  For offline use: set FRANKENSEARCH_MODEL_DIR to a pre-populated cache
```

### No Index Found
```
error: no search index found
  looked in: /home/user/project/.frankensearch/

  To fix: run `fsfs index /home/user/project` to create an index
```

### Permission Errors
```
error: cannot write to index directory: /home/user/project/.frankensearch/
  reason: Permission denied (os error 13)

  To fix: check directory permissions or use --index-dir to specify an alternative
```

### ONNX Runtime Errors
```
error: failed to load ONNX model: potion-128m
  reason: ONNX Runtime version mismatch (expected >=1.14, found 1.12)

  To fix: update frankensearch to the latest version: fsfs update
```

## Implementation

Use the existing `frankensearch-core/src/error.rs` error types but add user-facing
`Display` implementations that include context and fix suggestions. The `miette`
crate (if added) can provide even nicer error formatting with source spans.

## DCG Pattern

DCG has excellent error messages that always include:
1. What happened (error)
2. Why it matters (context)
3. What to do about it (fix suggestion)

We follow the same three-part pattern for every error.

 {"id":"bd-2w7x.32","title":"Layered configuration system (env > project > user > defaults)","description":"Implement DCG-style layered configuration with clear precedence: (1) Environment variables (highest): FRANKENSEARCH_* prefix for all config fields. FRANKENSEARCH_MODEL_DIR, FRANKENSEARCH_QUALITY_WEIGHT, FRANKENSEARCH_RRF_K, FRANKENSEARCH_FAST_ONLY, FRANKENSEARCH_NO_COLOR, FRANKENSEARCH_VERBOSE, FRANKENSEARCH_CHECK_UPDATES. (2) Project config: .frankensearch/config.toml in the project root (discovered by walking up to git root). (3) User config: XDG_CONFIG_HOME/frankensearch/config.toml or ~/.config/frankensearch/config.toml. (4) Compiled defaults (lowest): TwoTierConfig::default(). The 'fsfs config' subcommand should show the merged configuration with annotations showing where each value came from (env/project/user/default). Config format: TOML. Validate config on load, warn on unknown keys.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:14:36.563855557Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T17:25:57.629886451Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","feature","t5-package"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.32","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:14:36.563855557Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.32","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:57.418509363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1337,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.32","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design: Layered Configuration System

Configuration should be discoverable and predictable, following standard precedence:

```
CLI flags > Environment variables > Project config > User config > Defaults
```

## Configuration Locations

| Layer   | Location                                      | Format |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------|--------|
| CLI     | `--limit 20`, `--format json`                  | flags  |
| Env     | `FRANKENSEARCH_LIMIT=20`                       | env    |
| Project | `.frankensearch/config.toml`                   | TOML   |
| User    | `~/.config/frankensearch/config.toml`          | TOML   |
| Default | compiled-in defaults                           | Rust   |

## Existing Code

`frankensearch-fsfs/src/config.rs` already implements most of this:
- `FsfsConfig` struct with all configuration fields
- `PROFILE_PRECEDENCE_CHAIN` defines the resolution order
- `load_from_sources()` loads and merges from all layers
- `ConfigSource` tracks where each value came from
- `ConfigWarning` reports issues during loading
- `ConfigLoadedEvent` for observability

## Configuration Discovery Command

```bash
fsfs config show          # Show effective config with sources
fsfs config show --json   # Machine-readable
fsfs config init          # Create a starter config file
fsfs config validate      # Check config for errors
```

Example `fsfs config show` output:
```
search.limit = 10                    (default)
search.fast_tier_model = "potion-128m"  (default)
search.rrf_k = 60                    (default)
indexing.batch_size = 512            (~/.config/frankensearch/config.toml)
indexing.exclude_patterns += ["*.log"]  (.frankensearch/config.toml)
```

## Environment Variable Naming

Convention: `FRANKENSEARCH_` prefix + SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE section + key:
- `FRANKENSEARCH_SEARCH_LIMIT=20`
- `FRANKENSEARCH_INDEXING_BATCH_SIZE=512`
- `FRANKENSEARCH_MODEL_DIR=/custom/path`

Special shortcuts (no section prefix):
- `FRANKENSEARCH_OFFLINE=1` — disable all network access
- `FRANKENSEARCH_VERBOSE=1` — enable verbose logging
- `FRANKENSEARCH_COLOR=never` — disable colors
","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:25:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2w7x.33","title":"README with one-liner install and quick-start guide","description":"Create a polished README.md that gets a user from zero to searching in 60 seconds. Structure: (1) One-liner install: curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --easy-mode. (2) Quick start: 3 commands (install, index, search) with example output. (3) What it does: 2-tier hybrid search combining fast approximate + quality semantic + lexical BM25. (4) Features list: auto-download models, watch mode, streaming for AI agents, explain results, colored output. (5) Configuration: env vars, config file, command flags. (6) How it works: brief architecture (potion-128M -> RRF -> MiniLM -> FlashRank). (7) Comparison with alternatives: why not just grep/ripgrep/ctags? (semantic understanding, not just pattern matching). (8) FAQ. (9) Contributing. (10) License. Use shields.io badges for build status, crates.io version, license. No emojis unless the user requests them. Keep it under 300 lines — link to detailed docs for depth.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltGlen","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:14:49.265413901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:33.602974787Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:33.602956503Z","close_reason":"Completed README quick-start rewrite","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","readme","t6-docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.33","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:14:49.265413901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.33","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:16:03.766729067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1338,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.33","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design: README with One-Liner Install

The README is the first thing a potential user sees. It must convey value in 10 seconds
and get them to a working search in 60 seconds.

## Structure

1. **Hero section**: One-sentence description + key stats (search speed, accuracy)
2. **Install**: `curl -fsSL ... | bash` one-liner front and center
3. **Quick start**: 3 commands to go from zero to search results
4. **Features**: Bullet list of key capabilities
5. **How it works**: Brief architecture overview (2-tier, RRF fusion)
6. **Configuration**: Link to config docs
7. **Comparison**: How does this compare to ripgrep, grep.app, etc.
8. **Contributing**: How to build from source, run tests

## Quick Start Section

```bash
# Install
curl -fsSL https://frankensearch.dev/install.sh | bash

# Index your project
fsfs index ./my-project

# Search
fsfs search "how does authentication work"
```

That's it. Three commands. Under 60 seconds from first contact to useful results.

## Key Messaging

- "2-tier hybrid search" — fast semantic + lexical, quality refinement in background
- "Works offline after first run" — models are cached locally
- "Agent-friendly" — NDJSON streaming, stable result IDs, explain command
- "Zero config" — sensible defaults, works out of the box
- "Privacy-first" — everything local, no cloud, no telemetry

## Visual Elements

- ASCII art logo (small, 3-4 lines max)
- GIF/SVG screencast showing the search experience
- Badge row: CI status, version, license, downloads

## DCG README Reference

DCG's README is well-structured with a clear value proposition, one-liner install,
and extensive feature documentation. We follow the same structure but tailored
for search use cases rather than command safety.
","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:26:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2w7x.34","title":"Architecture overview document","description":"Create docs/architecture.md explaining the system design for contributors and curious users. Cover: (1) Crate map: 12 crates with one-line descriptions and dependency arrows (text diagram). (2) Data flow: document -> canonicalize -> embed (fast) -> FSVI index. Query -> parse -> classify -> embed -> search FSVI + Tantivy -> RRF fuse -> optionally embed (quality) -> blend -> optionally rerank -> results. (3) The 2-tier strategy: WHY we use two embedders (latency vs quality tradeoff, progressive results). (4) Storage: FrankenSQLite for metadata, FSVI for vectors, Tantivy for lexical. (5) Durability: RaptorQ FEC sidecars for corruption detection/repair. (6) Async runtime: asupersync (NOT tokio) — explain why and what it means. (7) Key design decisions with rationale: f16 quantization, RRF K=60, progressive iterator, NaN-safe ordering. This document should make a new contributor productive within one reading.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltGlen","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:14:58.198710473Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T18:33:47.407280914Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T18:33:47.407262610Z","close_reason":"Completed architecture overview docs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","docs","t6-docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.34","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:14:58.198710473Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.34","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:16:03.931616774Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1339,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.34","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design: Architecture Overview Document

A technical document for contributors and advanced users explaining how frankensearch
works internally.

## Sections

1. **Crate Architecture**: 12-crate workspace diagram showing dependencies
2. **Search Pipeline**: Query -> Parse -> Classify -> Embed -> Retrieve -> Fuse -> Rank -> Display
3. **Two-Tier Architecture**: Fast tier (potion-128M) vs quality tier (MiniLM-L6-v2)
4. **Fusion**: RRF (K=60) combining BM25 lexical + semantic cosine similarity
5. **Progressive Iterator**: SearchPhase::Initial -> Refined -> RefinementFailed
6. **Storage**: FrankenSQLite-backed document store with content-hash dedup
7. **Durability**: RaptorQ FEC sidecars for corruption detection/repair
8. **Index Format**: FSVI binary format with f16 quantization and HNSW
9. **Text Processing**: NFC normalization, markdown strip, code collapse
10. **Configuration**: Layered config resolution chain

## Audience

- Contributors who need to understand the codebase
- Advanced users who want to tune search quality
- Agents that need to understand the system to debug issues

## Format

Markdown document in `docs/architecture/overview.md`. Should include Mermaid diagrams
for the crate dependency graph and search pipeline flow.

## Existing Docs

The `docs/architecture/` directory already has some documents from previous sessions.
This bead consolidates and polishes them into a coherent architecture overview.

## Relationship to Beads

The beads system (bd-3un, bd-3w1, bd-2hz, bd-2yu, bd-2w7x) contains extremely
detailed design documentation in bead descriptions and comments. The architecture
doc should reference beads where appropriate but should be readable standalone
without knowledge of the beads system.

 {"id":"bd-2w7x.45","title":"fsfs download-models: explicit model cache management command","description":"/tmp/bd-2w7x.45-desc.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:34.582397303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T18:33:37.615849120Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","feature","model","t2-cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.45","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:34.582397303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.45","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:51.382556681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1369,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.45","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why This Is a Separate Command

The offline fallback bead (.8) describes the degradation hierarchy when models are
missing. But the user needs a way to PROACTIVELY manage models:
- Pre-populate cache before going offline
- Verify cache integrity after disk issues
- List what's available and what's missing
- Force re-download of a corrupt model

Auto-download (.6) handles the "just works" case. This command handles the "I need
to manage models explicitly" case.

## Existing Code to Build On

- `model_download.rs` has `ModelDownloader` with `download_file()` method
- `model_manifest.rs` has `ModelManifest` and `ModelEntry` types
- `model_cache.rs` (new file) has cache resolution logic from bd-2w7x.4
- `model_registry.rs` has `ModelRegistry::resolve()` for name resolution

## Integration Points

- The --list flag inspects the cache directory and reads manifest entries
- The --verify flag calls the same integrity check from bd-2w7x.7
- The --output flag overrides the cache dir for export to a different location
- The --force flag bypasses the "already cached" check

## Test Plan

This command needs its own tests in the CLI test suite (.42):
- `fsfs download-models --list` with empty cache: shows all models as MISSING
- `fsfs download-models --list` with full cache: shows all models as CACHED
- `fsfs download-models --verify` with valid cache: all OK
- `fsfs download-models --verify` with corrupt model: reports MISMATCH
- `fsfs download-models --output /tmp/models/` exports to custom dir

 {"id":"bd-2w7x.46","title":"Unit tests for auto-update: self-update, version cache, backup/rollback","description":"/tmp/bd-2w7x.46-desc.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:35.685218690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T18:33:37.779890220Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["t4-update","test","unit-test","update"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.46","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:35.685218690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.46","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:51.542063161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.46","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:51.699737699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1370,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.46","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Mock Infrastructure

Auto-update tests are challenging because they interact with:
1. GitHub Releases API (network)
2. The current binary on disk (filesystem)
3. Version comparison logic (pure logic)
4. Backup/rollback file operations (filesystem)

## Test Strategy

### Pure Logic Tests (no mocking needed)
- Version parsing: "0.2.0" < "0.3.0" < "1.0.0"
- Version parsing with pre-release: "0.3.0-rc1" < "0.3.0"
- Semver comparison edge cases

### Mock-Based Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_update_check_detects_newer_version() {
    let _guard = tracing_test::init_subscriber();
    let mock_server = MockServer::start().await;

    // Return a mock release response
    Mock::given(method("GET"))
        .and(path("/repos/owner/repo/releases/latest"))
        .respond_with(ResponseTemplate::new(200).set_body_json(json!({
            "tag_name": "v0.3.0",
            "assets": [{"name": "fsfs-linux-x86_64", "browser_download_url": "..."}]
        })))
        .mount(&mock_server).await;

    let result = check_for_update(&mock_server.uri(), "0.2.0");
    assert!(result.update_available);
    assert_eq!(result.latest_version, "0.3.0");
}
```

### Filesystem Tests
- Use tempdir for all binary operations
- Test atomic rename: verify no partial writes during replacement
- Test backup directory: verify rotation (keep 3, delete oldest)
- Test rollback: verify byte-for-byte restoration

## Logging Requirements

Every test logs:
- Current version, detected version, decision (update/skip/rollback)
- File paths for binary, backup, temp locations
- API response details (status code, body preview)
- Timing for each operation phase
","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:33:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2w7x.47","title":"E2E installer smoke test: install, index, search, verify, uninstall lifecycle","description":"/tmp/bd-2w7x.47-desc.md","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:38.487374451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T18:33:37.941473026Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critical","e2e","installer","t7-test","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.47","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:38.487374451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.47","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.37","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:52.034473737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.47","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.43","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:51.861671624Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1371,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.47","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why a Full Lifecycle Test

Individual unit tests verify components in isolation. The e2e test (.37) verifies
search quality. But neither tests the INSTALL experience end-to-end. This test
validates the complete user journey in a clean environment:

1. Start with nothing (clean HOME directory)
2. Run the installer
3. Index a project
4. Search and get results
5. Run doctor
6. Uninstall
7. Verify everything is cleaned up

This catches integration bugs that unit tests miss: PATH setup failures, incomplete
uninstall, model download during index, config file discovery issues, etc.

## Test Isolation

The test creates a completely isolated environment:
```bash
TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)
export HOME="$TMPDIR/home"
export PATH="$TMPDIR/install/bin:$PATH"
export XDG_DATA_HOME="$TMPDIR/home/.local/share"
export XDG_CONFIG_HOME="$TMPDIR/home/.config"
```

This ensures the test never touches the real user's home directory, models, or config.

## Failure Diagnosis Protocol

When any step fails:
1. **Exit code** identifies the phase (install=1, index=2, search=3, uninstall=4)
2. **Log files** are dumped to stderr for CI
3. **Directory listings** show the state of .frankensearch/ and model cache
4. **Doctor output** provides system diagnostics (if binary is installed)

This makes CI failures actionable — you can see exactly what happened without
reproducing locally.

## CI Integration

```yaml
- name: E2E Installer Smoke Test
  run: bash tests/installer/e2e_smoke.sh
  timeout-minutes: 5
  env:
    FRANKENSEARCH_LOG: debug
```

## Relationship to Other Test Beads

- bd-2w7x.37 tests search recall quality (is the search good?)
- bd-2w7x.43 tests installer scripts in isolation (does install.sh work?)
- This bead (.47) tests the FULL lifecycle (does the whole thing work together?)
","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:33:37Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2w7x.48","title":"fsfs status: show index health, model state, and config at a glance","description":"/tmp/bd-2w7x.48-desc.md","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"SilverBeacon","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:39.719471303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T18:52:52.527374212Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","feature","t2-cli","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.48","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:39.719471303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.48","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:52.356388589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.48","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:31:52.193827080Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1372,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.48","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why This Command Is Essential

Every mature CLI tool has a status command:
- `git status` — what's changed?
- `docker ps` — what's running?
- `kubectl get pods` — what's the state?

`fsfs status` answers the most common user questions:
- "Is my index up to date?" → shows stale file count
- "Are models downloaded?" → shows model cache state
- "What config am I using?" → shows config sources
- "How big is the index?" → shows index size breakdown

## Machine-Readable Output

The --format json output is designed for agent consumption. An AI agent can
run `fsfs status --format json` and parse the result to determine whether to
suggest re-indexing, model download, or config changes.

## Existing Code to Build On

- `frankensearch-storage/src/metrics.rs` has `StorageMetrics` for file/size counts
- `frankensearch-fusion/src/staleness.rs` has staleness detection logic
- `frankensearch-embed/src/model_registry.rs` has model name resolution
- `frankensearch-fsfs/src/config.rs` has config source tracking
- `frankensearch-core/src/generation.rs` has generation tracking for cache invalidation

## UX Details

- Show human-friendly time ("2 hours ago", "yesterday") not raw timestamps
- Show human-friendly sizes ("45 MB", "1.2 GB") not raw bytes
- Use color: green for OK, yellow for warnings (stale), red for errors (missing)
- Keep output concise — one line per concern, no verbose prose

## Dependencies

Needs .28 (entry point) for clap registration and .14 (config) for config display.
Doesn't need .9/.10 (index/search) because it reads index metadata directly.
","created_at":"2026-02-14T18:33:38Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2w7x.5","title":"Model manifest with download URLs and SHA-256 checksums","description":"Create a compiled-in model manifest (model_manifest.rs or similar) that maps model names to their download metadata. For each model: HuggingFace repo URL, list of required files (model.onnx, tokenizer.json, config.json), SHA-256 checksum per file, total download size, model dimensions, and recommended use (fast/quality/reranker). The manifest is the single source of truth for what models frankensearch needs. It must be versioned so that model upgrades can be detected (if the manifest changes, re-download). Initial models: (1) potion-base-128M from mixedbread-ai (256d, ~50MB ONNX), (2) all-MiniLM-L6-v2 from sentence-transformers (384d, ~90MB ONNX), (3) ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2 from cross-encoder (reranker, ~90MB ONNX). Note: the existing model_registry.rs and model_manifest.rs in frankensearch-embed may already have partial implementations — audit and extend rather than rewrite.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"CobaltFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:11:17.653432551Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T17:49:29.847421298Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T17:49:29.847399477Z","close_reason":"Manifest metadata enriched with pinned revisions + checksums","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["feature","model","t1-model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:11:17.653432551Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:52.201102336Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1310,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design: Model Manifest Format

A model manifest is a JSON file that describes a model's identity, download location,
expected checksums, and compatibility requirements. This is the single source of truth
for what models frankensearch knows about and where to get them.

```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "models": [
    {
      "name": "potion-128m",
      "version": "v1",
      "display_name": "Potion Base 128M (fast tier)",
      "description": "256-dim sentence embeddings, ~0.57ms per query",
      "dimension": 256,
      "files": [
        {
          "name": "model.onnx",
          "url": "https://huggingface.co/AlessandroFranwordi/potion-base-128M/resolve/main/onnx/model.onnx",
          "sha256": "abc123...",
          "size_bytes": 30000000
        },
        {
          "name": "tokenizer.json",
          "url": "https://huggingface.co/AlessandroFranwordi/potion-base-128M/resolve/main/tokenizer.json",
          "sha256": "def456...",
          "size_bytes": 500000
        }
      ],
      "tier": "fast",
      "onnx_runtime_version": ">=1.14.0"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Bundling Strategy

The manifest should be compiled into the binary as a `const &str` (via `include_str!`).
This means the binary always knows what models it needs without network access. The
manifest in the cache directory is for version tracking / cache invalidation only.

## Versioning and Updates

When we release a new frankensearch version with updated models, the embedded manifest
changes. On first run, the binary compares its embedded manifest against the cached
manifest. If models are missing or checksums don't match, it triggers auto-download
(bd-2w7x.6). This is how DCG handles its precommit hook binary — embedded checksums
that drive cache validation.

## HuggingFace URLs

Both potion-128M and MiniLM-L6-v2 are hosted on HuggingFace. The download URLs use
the `/resolve/main/` path which gives direct file download (not the git LFS redirect).
Mirror URLs can be added for reliability (e.g., a GitHub Releases mirror).

## Existing Code

`crates/frankensearch-embed/src/model_manifest.rs` already has ModelManifest and
ModelEntry types. These need to be enriched with download URLs, checksums, and
the schema version field. The file already has the right structure — this bead
extends it rather than replacing it.
","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:20:39Z"},{"id":1351,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.5","author":"CobaltFalcon","text":"Claimed this bead. Next step is auditing current manifest/registry/download code, then extending manifest metadata (URLs/checksums/filesize/schema version/tier info) and wiring versioning checks needed for auto-download flow.","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:35:00Z"},{"id":1353,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.5","author":"CobaltFalcon","text":"Completed manifest enrichment in crates/frankensearch-embed/src/model_manifest.rs with production-ready metadata: pinned commit revisions, explicit HuggingFace resolve URLs, concrete SHA-256 digests, byte sizes, tier metadata, version/description fields, and precomputed total_size_bytes (download_size_bytes field serialized as total_size_bytes). Built-in catalog schema bumped to 2 and constructors now produce production-ready manifests for potion multilingual 128M, all-MiniLM-L6-v2, and ms-marco MiniLM reranker. Updated model_download tests for non-production-ready rejection using explicit placeholder overrides, and added metadata integrity tests. Validation run: rch exec -- cargo check -p frankensearch-embed --all-targets; rch exec -- cargo test -p frankensearch-embed model_manifest -- --nocapture; rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankensearch-embed --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo fmt -p frankensearch-embed --check.","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:49:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2w7x.6","title":"Auto-download models on first embed() call with progress reporting","description":"When an embedder is first invoked and the required ONNX model is not found in the cache directory, automatically download it from HuggingFace. Critical requirements: (1) Use asupersync HTTP client (NOT reqwest/tokio — project-wide mandate), specifically asupersync::http::h1 + asupersync/tls via the download feature flag. (2) Show download progress to stderr with file size, download speed, and ETA — use a simple progress bar that works in both interactive terminals and piped output. (3) Downloads must be atomic: write to a .tmp file, verify SHA-256 checksum, then rename to final path. This prevents corrupt partial downloads from being used. (4) Support concurrent downloads (fast + quality models can download in parallel). (5) Emit structured tracing events for observability (model name, size, duration, outcome). (6) The download should happen transparently — the user just runs 'fsfs search' and models appear. First search will be slow (download time) but subsequent searches use the cache.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:11:26.070200448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T18:37:19.252650602Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T18:29:24.935070491Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critical","feature","model","t1-model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:11:26.070200448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:52.364911733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1311,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design: Auto-Download on First Embed Call

The user experience goal: a developer runs `fsfs index ./my-project` for the first time.
They have no models on disk. The embedder detects this and automatically downloads the
required model(s) with a progress bar, then proceeds to index. Total time from install
to first search: under 60 seconds on broadband.

## Download Protocol

1. Check cache (bd-2w7x.4) for model directory
2. If missing or manifest mismatch, consult embedded manifest (bd-2w7x.5)
3. Download each file to a temp location within the cache directory
4. Verify SHA-256 checksum (bd-2w7x.7)
5. Atomic rename into final location
6. Load model and proceed

## Progress Reporting

Use stderr for progress so stdout remains clean for piping:
```
Downloading potion-128m model (30 MB)...
[================>                 ] 52% 15.6 MB/s ETA 1s
```

For non-TTY environments (CI, pipes), emit periodic status lines:
```
Downloading potion-128m model... 25%... 50%... 75%... done (30 MB in 2s)
```

## asupersync Requirement (CRITICAL)

Downloads MUST use `asupersync::http::h1` + `asupersync/tls`. NOT reqwest, NOT hyper.
This is a project-wide architectural constraint. The download function signature:

```rust
async fn download_model(cx: &Cx, manifest: &ModelEntry, cache_dir: &Path) -> Result<()>
```

The `cx: &Cx` parameter provides the async runtime context. See
`crates/frankensearch-embed/src/model_download.rs` for the existing download stub.

## Existing Code to Build On

- `model_download.rs` has `download_file()` and `ModelDownloader` — needs asupersync
  HTTP integration and progress callbacks
- `model_registry.rs` has `ModelRegistry::resolve()` — needs to trigger download
  when resolution returns a cache miss
- The `download` feature flag in Cargo.toml gates the download capability:
  `download = ['asupersync/tls']`

## DCG Parallel

DCG's install.sh downloads a prebuilt binary with `curl -fsSL` and verifies SHA-256.
We're doing the same but for ONNX model files instead of binaries. The progress bar
pattern from DCG's installer (using gum for TTY, plain text for non-TTY) applies to
our model download UX too.

## Opt-Out

Environment variable `FRANKENSEARCH_OFFLINE=1` disables auto-download. This is for
air-gapped environments. See bd-2w7x.8 for the full offline fallback design.

 {"id":"bd-2w7x.7","title":"Model integrity verification on load","description":"When loading a cached model from disk, verify its SHA-256 checksum against the manifest before passing it to the ONNX runtime. This catches: (1) corrupted downloads that passed the initial check but degraded on disk, (2) tampered model files, (3) version mismatches where the manifest was updated but old model files remain. On checksum mismatch: log a warning, delete the corrupt file, and re-trigger auto-download. The verification should be fast — SHA-256 of a 90MB file takes ~200ms which is acceptable at startup. Cache the verification result in a .verified file (containing the checksum + timestamp) so subsequent loads skip re-hashing if the file mtime hasn't changed.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:11:30.289260614Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T17:20:39.974037362Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["feature","integrity","model","t1-model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:11:30.289260614Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2w7x.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T17:15:52.525872176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1312,"issue_id":"bd-2w7x.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design: Model Integrity Verification

Every model file gets SHA-256 verified at two points:
1. **After download** — compare computed hash against manifest (bd-2w7x.5)
2. **On load** — optionally re-verify when loading from cache (configurable, default off
   for speed, on for first load after update)

## Hash File Format

Each model file gets a companion `.sha256` file in the cache directory:
```
abc123def456...  model.onnx
```
Standard GNU coreutils format, so `sha256sum -c model.onnx.sha256` works.

## Implementation

```rust
fn verify_integrity(path: &Path, expected_sha256: &str) -> Result<bool> {
    let mut hasher = Sha256::new();
    let mut file = File::open(path)?;
    io::copy(&mut file, &mut hasher)?;
    let computed = hex::encode(hasher.finalize());
    Ok(computed == expected_sha256)
}
```

Use the `sha2` crate (already in workspace for frankensearch-storage content-hash dedup).

## Failure Modes

- Hash mismatch after download: delete the corrupt file, retry download once, then error
- Hash mismatch on load: warn user, suggest `fsfs doctor` (bd-2w7x.40) to re-download
- Missing .sha256 file: treat as unverified, log warning, but don't block operation

## Why This Matters

ONNX model files are opaque binary blobs. A truncated or corrupt model file will cause
mysterious runtime failures (ORT panics, wrong embeddings, NaN scores). Catching
corruption early with checksums saves hours of debugging. This is especially important
for models downloaded over flaky networks or stored on unreliable storage.

## Security Note

SHA-256 verification protects against accidental corruption, NOT against malicious
supply chain attacks. For that, we'd need signature verification (sigstore/cosign).
That's out of scope for v1 but noted as a future enhancement.
